sn20.8:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying was staying near Vesālī, at the Gabled Hall in the Great Forest.
sn20.8:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
sn20.8:2.1: “The Licchavis now live heedfully, ardent in exercise, with blocks of wood for pillows.
sn20.8:2.2: King Ajātasattu of Māgadha, Vedehi’s son, doesn’t gain an opening among them, doesn’t gain a foothold.
sn20.8:2.3: But there will come a time in the future when the Licchavis grow delicate, with soft palms of their hands & soles of their feet.
sn20.8:2.4: They will sleep until sunrise on soft beds with pillows of cotton wool.
sn20.8:2.5: King Ajātasattu of Māgadha, Vedehi’s son, will gain an opening among them, will gain a foothold.
sn20.8:3.1: “The monks now live heedfully, ardent in exertion, with blocks of wood for pillows.
sn20.8:3.2: Māra the Evil One doesn’t gain an opening among them, doesn’t gain a foothold.
sn20.8:3.3: But there will come a time in the future when the monks grow delicate, with soft palms of their hands & soles of their feet.
sn20.8:3.4: They will sleep until sunrise on soft beds with pillows of cotton wool.
sn20.8:3.5: Māra the Evil One will gain an opening among them, will gain a foothold.
sn20.8:3.7: ‘We will live heedfully, ardent in exertion, with blocks of wood for pillows.’
sn20.8:3.8: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
